title: Python Exceptions and Files
description: Practice reading a file with a handled failure path.
learningGoals: Use with to close a file automatically.

completionChecks.prompt: Which exception does this code handle?
completionChecks.explanation: The except clause catches FileNotFoundError when notes.txt is absent.

completionChecks.prompt: Why use with open(...) as file?
choices: It converts the file to a list
completionChecks.explanation: The context manager closes the file when the block exits.

targetCode:
# Learn: Handle a missing file without hiding all errors.
try:
    with open("notes.txt", encoding="utf-8") as file:
        text = file.read()  # The file closes after this block.
except FileNotFoundError:
    text = ""
print(len(text))
